body.title: Narrow 'pre-flight blocked (no recognizer)' consult trigger — unactionable hypotheses
body.result: Removed _maybe_consult_stuck(preflight_unrelated) from the no-recognizer-matched pre-flight branch. Post-heal failure path retains it. Test updated to assert absence. No brainstem edit needed.
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Consult observe-and-tune Finding 4: 'pre-flight blocked (no recognizer)' fires on every red-preflight but produces the same 'test suite is broken, unrelated to your code' hypothesis every time. Unactionable — Igor has no mechanism to act on 'not your problem, go look at infra'. Either gate the trigger on a second signal (e.g., pre-flight fails AND git status shows changes to test-relevant files) or remove the trigger entirely.

Related: this is EXACTLY the stuck-loop that's blocking Igor on T-pe-chain-via-uc-proxy today — same consult fires every 6 minutes, same hypothesis.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (_maybe_consult_stuck pre-flight hook site), tests/test_pe_chain_consult_hooks.py.
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** IN — decide: narrow the trigger OR remove it. OUT — building a new preflight-analysis consult (that's a bigger design).
**Test plan:** if narrowed, new test covers both fire and no-fire sides. If removed, assertion the hook no longer fires on no-recognizer pre-flight fails.
